

## **Part 1: Setting Up Your Google Cloud Credentials**

1. Go to the Google Cloud Console:  
    [https://console.cloud.google.com/](https://console.cloud.google.com/)  
2. In the top-left corner, click the **three horizontal lines (menu icon)**.  
3. From the sidebar:  
   * Select **“APIs & Services”**  
   * Then click **“Credentials”**

![][image1]

### **Set Up OAuth Consent Screen**

4. If you haven’t created an OAuth consent screen yet, you’ll be prompted to do so:  
   * Enter an **App Name** (can be anything)  
   * Enter your **Email**  
   * Follow the prompts and complete the required fields

![][image2]  
![][image3]  
 

### **Create OAuth Client ID**

5. After finishing the consent screen setup:  
   * Go back to **Credentials**  
   * Click **“Create Credentials”**  
   * Select **“OAuth Client ID”**  
6. Choose the appropriate application type (e.g., Web application, Desktop app, etc.)

![][image4]  
![][image5]

***Now copy these credentials exactly.*** 

![][image6]

![][image7]

Part 2: Changing your client ID in your Vs Code.

**Code base**

* In you CCCsite folder navigate to the account folder  
  * ![][image8]  
* Navigate templates folder then to sign-in file  
  * Ctrl-f and look for data-client\_id  
  * Replace the credential with your credential in google cloud  
  * ![][image9]  
  * ![][image10]  
* Navigate to the sign-up file in the same folder  
  * Ctrl-f and look for data-client\_id  
  * **Once again** replace the client\_id with your credential in google cloud  
    * ![][image11]  
* Finally navigate to your views.py located outside your templates folder, but in account folder  
  * Ctrl-f GOOGLE\_CLIENT\_ID  
  * ![][image12]  
  * Once more repeat the process of replacing the client id with your credentials.  
* If all these steps are met you will be authorized to sign in to the website.
